title: Error::check casts i32 to u32 for TpmRc — negative codes appear as large hex values
description: wolftpm/src/error.rs Error::check does TpmRc(rc as u32). wolfTPM returns some internal error codes as negative i32 values (e.g. -100 = BAD_FUNC_ARG mapped to 0xFFFFFF9C). The tpm_rc_name table knows about 0xFFFFFF9C (TPM_RC_TIMEOUT) but only one such code. Other negative wolfTPM internal codes (BAD_FUNC_ARG = -173, etc.) will Display as 'TPM error 0xffffff53' with no symbolic name, which is misleading — it looks like a TPM2 spec error code but is actually a wolfTPM library internal code. Users filing issues will paste the hex without knowing it's a wolfTPM internal code. The wolfTPM error space should be documented (the distinction between TPM2-spec RCs < 0x1000 and wolfTPM internal codes < 0) and more wolfTPM internal codes should be named, or TpmRc should keep the i32 and display negative values separately.
